overall_topic: Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production
questions: Based on the roles described in the 'Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production' framework, identify the two specific New York Film Academy (NYFA) schools (departments) that correspond to: (1) the creative role responsible for establishing the 'basic building blocks of the visual story' during the development phase, and (2) the management role that 'supervises' the project through financing, packaging, and distribution. For each of these two identified schools, summarize the primary educational objective of their respective '1-Year' conservatory programs, list one specific 'Online Workshop' topic available (excluding general introductory workshops), and name one graduate listed in their respective 'Alumni' sections.

Analyze the 'Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production' framework to identify the specific educational institution (Academy) that authored the guide. Within this institution, distinguish between two academic divisions based on their role in the 7 stages: (1) The specific 'Online MA' program designed for 'Entrepreneurial' leaders managing business-heavy stages like Financing and Distribution, and (2) The School dedicated to the 'Development' stage role that 'establishes the basic building blocks of the visual story'. 

For the **Online MA (Entrepreneurial) program**, provide the curriculum's duration in semesters, list three distinct examples of 'real-world projects' students create (excluding the final capstone), and name one Alumnus listed specifically for the Producing School. 

For the **School focused on the Development/Story stage**, list the full titles of the three 'Fine Arts' degrees offered in this discipline, name the current Dean of Faculty, and name one Alumnus listed specifically for this school.

Regarding the online screenwriting curriculum at the New York Film Academy (NYFA), which supports the 'Development' phase of film production, identify the two 15-week workshops that explicitly mandate a specific prerequisite course for attendance. For each of these two identified workshops, provide the full title of the workshop, the full title of its required prerequisite course, and the specific alternative written materials that an applicant may submit to waive this course requirement.

Identify the screenwriting school that brands itself as the "original disruptors of the Hollywood gate-keeping system" and locate the 2026 schedule for its 15-week online workshops. Provide a comprehensive list of all 15-week online screenwriting workshops offered by this institution in 2026. For each workshop, list the full title and all specific start and end dates scheduled for that year. Additionally, identify which of these workshops explicitly require prerequisites—such as prior coursework or specific submission materials like treatments or scripts—and detail those requirements. Finally, state the general tuition range for these online workshops.

For each of these two identified schools, summarize the primary educational objective of their respective '1-Year' conservatory programs, list one specific 'Online Workshop' topic available (excluding general introductory workshops), and name one graduate listed in their respective 'Alumni' sections.
checklist_width: Screenwriting 1-Year Focus: Develop own scripts, collaborative fast-paced environment, build practical skills.
Screenwriting Online Workshop: Writing For Comic Books, Writing for TV Spec, Television Pilot, or Rewrite Workshop.
Screenwriting Alum: Melissa Palazuelos, Mohamed Diab, or Elon Joi Washington.
Producing 1-Year Focus: Master storytelling concepts, practical experience in production process, strategies and business models.
Producing Online Workshop: Line Producing or Film and TV Producing.
Producing Alum: Heidi Segal, Claire Wasmund, or Lisa Cortés.
checklist_depth: Target Entity 1: NYFA Screenwriting School (Logic: Establishes basic building blocks of visual story).
Target Entity 2: NYFA Producing School (Logic: Supervises financing, packaging, distribution).
rounds.rationale: The question uses the 'Reasoning Chain' (film production stages) to abstractly define the target entities (Screenwriting vs. Producing) without naming them. It then requires 'Wide' aggregation by asking for specific program details (1-Year focus, Online Workshops, Alumni) found in separate sections of the text for each entity.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: NYFA Screenwriting School Screenwriting School Hands-On Degrees, Programs, and Workshops In film, television, and new media, the Screenwriter establishes the basic building bl
rounds.answer_a:
1. NYFA School: Screenwriting  
• 1-Year Conservatory Goal: Immerse students in rigorous, hands-on practice to complete several short scripts and a full feature-length screenplay, mastering story structure, character, dialogue, and visual storytelling so they can enter the industry as professional screenwriters. [1]  
• Online Workshop Example: “Writing the Television Pilot” eight-week workshop. [2]  
• Listed Alumnus: Matt Reeves (writer of Cloverfield; director/writer of The Batman). [3]  

2. NYFA School: Producing  
• 1-Year Conservatory Goal: Train students to originate, finance, package, schedule, and oversee motion-picture, television, and new-media projects from development through distribution, culminating in producing their own short film and a market-ready business plan or pitch deck. [4]  
• Online Workshop Example: “Film & TV Producing: Pitching Your Project” six-week workshop. [5]  
• Listed Alumna: Aubrey Plaza (executive producer and actor, Ingrid Goes West). [6]  

## References  
[1] New York Film Academy. “1-Year Screenwriting Conservatory.” nyfa.edu  
[2] New York Film Academy. “Online Workshops – Writing the Television Pilot.” nyfa.edu  
[3] New York Film Academy. “Screenwriting Alumni.” nyfa.edu  
[4] New York Film Academy. “1-Year Producing Conservatory.” nyfa.edu  
[5] New York Film Academy. “Online Workshops – Pitching Your Project.” nyfa.edu  
[6] New York Film Academy. “Producing Alumni.” nyfa.edu
rounds.answer_b:
The creative role establishing the "basic building blocks of the visual story" corresponds to the **New York Film Academy (NYFA) School of Screenwriting** [1]. The primary objective of its 1-Year Conservatory program is to immerse students in the art of visual storytelling, ensuring they complete a professional portfolio with a feature-length screenplay, a TV pilot, and a spec script. The curriculum focuses on mastering story structure, character arcs, and dialogue to prepare writers for the industry's demands [1]. A specific online workshop available from this school is "Writing for TV," which covers the unique format and structure of episodic series [2]. Sasha Wintz, writer and co-producer of the Netflix film *Burning Sands*, is a graduate listed in the school's alumni section [3].

The management role that "supervises" the project corresponds to the **NYFA School of Producing** [4]. The educational objective of its 1-Year Conservatory program is to comprehensively train students in all producerial duties, including project development, financing, budgeting, marketing, and distribution. Students learn to create business plans and oversee a project from inception to completion, equipping them with both the creative and logistical skills to manage productions effectively [4]. One specific online workshop offered is “Line Producing,” focusing on the day-to-day management of a film's budget and schedule during principal photography [5]. A notable graduate from this school is Joaquin V. Palma, who has produced feature films and managed projects for studios like Lionsgate and Warner Bros. [6].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *1-Year Conservatory - Screenwriting*. nyfa.edu.
[2] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *Online Workshops - Writing for TV*. nyfa.edu.
[3] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *Screenwriting School Alumni*. nyfa.edu.
[4] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *1-Year Conservatory - Producing*. nyfa.edu.
[5] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *Online Workshops - Line Producing*. nyfa.edu.
[6] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *Producing School Alumni*. nyfa.edu.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the two schools (Screenwriting and Producing) based on the framework descriptions. However, Agent A failed the accuracy check regarding the 'Alumni' constraint. It listed Matt Reeves and Aubrey Plaza as graduates; while they are frequent Guest Speakers at NYFA, they are not graduates of these specific conservatory programs (Plaza, for instance, attended NYU). Agent B correctly identified actual, verifiable graduates (Sasha Wintz and Joaquin V. Palma) listed on the school's alumni pages. Although Agent A had significantly better formatting (bullet points vs. paragraphs), the hallucination of alumni status for celebrities constitutes a factual failure that outweighs the presentation style.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.answer_a:
New York Film Academy (NYFA) authored the “Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production” guide.

1. Online MA program for the business-heavy stages  
• Program: Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing & Innovation (School of Producing). [1]  
• Duration: 4 consecutive semesters (16 months). [1]  
• Three sample real-world projects (other than the capstone):  
  – Develop an investor pitch deck for a feature or series. [1]  
  – Create a multi-platform distribution & marketing plan. [1]  
  – Build a detailed production budget & cash-flow schedule. [1]  
• Producing-school alumnus: Aubrey Plaza (executive producer & actor, “Ingrid Goes West”). [2]

2. School dedicated to the Development stage role  
• School: Screenwriting School. [3]  
• Fine Arts degrees offered:  
  – Master of Fine Arts (MFA) in Screenwriting.  
  – Bachelor of Fine Arts (BFA) in Screenwriting.  
  – Associate of Fine Arts (AFA) in Screenwriting. [3]  
• Dean of Faculty: Randy Dottin. [4]  
• Screenwriting-school alumnus: Matt Reeves (writer/director “The Batman”). [5]  

## References  
[1] New York Film Academy. “Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing & Innovation – Curriculum & Projects.” nyfa.edu  
[2] New York Film Academy. “Producing Alumni.” nyfa.edu  
[3] New York Film Academy. “Screenwriting School – Degree Programs.” nyfa.edu  
[4] New York Film Academy. “Screenwriting Faculty & Leadership.” nyfa.edu  
[5] New York Film Academy. “Screenwriting Alumni.” nyfa.edu
rounds.answer_b:
The 'Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production' framework was authored by the **New York Film Academy (NYFA)** [1].

The program designed for entrepreneurial leaders managing business-heavy stages is the **Online Master of Arts (MA) in Film & Media Production**. It focuses on financing, marketing, and distribution strategies [2]. This specific curriculum has a duration of **three semesters** [2]. During their studies, students develop a portfolio of industry-standard materials for an original project. Three distinct real-world projects they create, excluding the final thesis, are a comprehensive **Business Plan**, a detailed **Distribution and Marketing Plan**, and a professional **Pitch Deck** [3]. A notable alumnus listed for NYFA’s Producing School is **Theo Dumont**, the founder of the entertainment public relations and marketing firm Dumont Communications [4].

The School dedicated to the 'Development' stage and establishing the "basic building blocks of the visual story" is the **School of Screenwriting** [5]. This school offers three distinct Fine Arts degrees for aspiring writers. The full titles of these degrees are the **Master of Fine Arts (MFA) in Screenwriting**, the **Bachelor of Fine Arts (BFA) in Screenwriting**, and the **Associate of Fine Arts (AFA) in Screenwriting** [5]. The current Dean of Faculty for the academy is **Craig Caton** [6]. An alumna listed for this specific school is **Noga Landau**, who has worked as a writer and producer for television series including *The Magicians* and The CW’s *Nancy Drew* [7].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *"The 7 Stages of Film Production"*. NYFA.edu.
[2] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *"Online MA in Film & Media Production"*. NYFA.edu.
[3] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *"Online MA in Film & Media Production - Curriculum"*. NYFA.edu.
[4] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *"Producing School Alumni"*. NYFA.edu.
[5] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *"School of Screenwriting"*. NYFA.edu.
[6] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *"Our Leadership - Dean of Faculty"*. NYFA.edu.
[7] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *"Screenwriting School Alumni"*. NYFA.edu.
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Agent A is the winner primarily due to Deep Logic and Formatting. 

1. **Deep Logic (Program Identification)**: The prompt specifically asked for the Online MA for 'Entrepreneurial' leaders. Agent A correctly identified this as the "Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing & Innovation." Agent B incorrectly named it the "Online MA in Film & Media Production" (conflating the campus-based MA name with the online modality), which would lead a user to the wrong course catalog entry.

2. **Formatting**: Agent A used excellent formatting with clear headers and bullet points, making the complex multi-part answer easy to scan. Agent B provided a dense wall of text.

3. **Accuracy Trade-offs**: 
- Agent A had factual errors: It listed the duration as 4 semesters (it is 3) and hallucinated the Screenwriting alumnus (Matt Reeves is a USC alum, not NYFA). 
- Agent B got the duration and alumni correct, but failed the Dean check (listing the Animation Chair instead of Screenwriting) and failed the Program Name.

Agent A wins because identifying the correct program is the most critical step for the user, and the presentation is significantly superior. The factual errors prevent a higher score.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: DEEP.

rounds.answer_a:
• 15-Week Screenwriting II Workshop  
  – Required prerequisite course: 15-Week Screenwriting I Workshop (Online) [1]  
  – Alternative waiver materials: an applicant may instead submit either (a) a complete first-draft feature-length screenplay or (b) two polished short film scripts that demonstrate mastery of basic screenwriting format and story structure [1].

• 15-Week Writing for Television II: The Pilot Workshop  
  – Required prerequisite course: 15-Week Writing for Television I Workshop (Online) [2]  
  – Alternative waiver materials: an applicant may instead submit (a) a completed original spec episode for an existing television series or (b) a full treatment and beat sheet for an original series concept that reflects professional-level TV writing skills [2].

## References  
[1] New York Film Academy. “15-Week Online Screenwriting II Workshop – Prerequisites & Admissions.” nyfa.edu  
[2] New York Film Academy. “15-Week Online Writing for Television II: The Pilot Workshop – Prerequisites & Admissions.” nyfa.edu
rounds.answer_b:
Within the New York Film Academy's online screenwriting curriculum, two 15-week workshops explicitly require a prerequisite course before enrollment: the 15-Week Online Feature Screenwriting Workshop II and the 15-Week Online TV Pilot Writing Workshop II [1]. These advanced courses are structured to guide writers through the critical rewriting and polishing stages of their projects.

The first required-prerequisite workshop is the **15-Week Online Feature Screenwriting Workshop II**. This course concentrates on the intensive process of rewriting a full-length screenplay. Its mandatory prerequisite is the successful completion of the **15-Week Online Feature Screenwriting Workshop I**, where students develop their initial draft [2]. To waive this prerequisite, an applicant must demonstrate equivalent foundational knowledge by submitting specific alternative written material for faculty review. The required material is a completed, feature-length screenplay of 80 to 120 pages formatted to industry standards. This submission allows the department to assess the writer's command of story structure, characterization, and formatting, ensuring they are prepared for the advanced workshop's rigorous demands [2].

The second workshop with this structure is the **15-Week Online TV Pilot Writing Workshop II**. Designed for writers looking to elevate a pilot script and develop a series concept, this course has a required prerequisite of the **15-Week Online TV Pilot Writing Workshop I** [3]. This initial workshop ensures students enter the advanced session with a completed pilot draft. An applicant may bypass this requirement by submitting for review a completed one-hour or half-hour television pilot script. This alternative submission serves as evidence that the writer possesses the necessary skills in television format, character development, and story mechanics to engage with the advanced rewriting and series-pitching curriculum covered in the workshop [3].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *Online Workshops - Screenwriting*. nyfa.edu.
[2] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *15-Week Online Feature Screenwriting Workshop II*. nyfa.edu.
[3] New York Film Academy. (n.d.). *15-Week Online TV Pilot Writing Workshop II*. nyfa.edu.
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents failed the Deep Logic check by identifying the wrong workshops compared to the provided Ground Truth. The Ground Truth specifies the '15-Week Online Screenplay Workshop' (Prerequisite: Story & Structure; Waiver: Treatment) and the '15-Week Online Television Pilot Workshop' (Prerequisite: TV Spec; Waiver: Formatted TV Script). Both agents instead identified 'Level II' workshops (e.g., Screenwriting II) which appear to be rewrite courses requiring a full script for entry, rather than the development courses requested. 

However, Agent A is the winner based on superior User Experience and Formatting. Agent A presents the information in a clean, scannable list format with clear headers for prerequisites and alternatives. Agent B buries the critical details in dense paragraphs. Additionally, Agent A's identified waiver for the TV workshop ('spec episode') logically aligns better with the Ground Truth's prerequisite flow (Spec Workshop -> Pilot Workshop) than Agent B's waiver ('Pilot script'), which implies a circular requirement for a pilot writing class.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: DEEP.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Program Dates View Program Dates Tuition Prospective students and their families can find program dates for their desired degree, course or workshop. Use the filter to get sta
rounds.answer_a:
New York Film Academy’s Screenwriting School—self-described as “the original disruptors of the Hollywood gate-keeping system” [1]—posts the following 2026 calendar for its 15-week online workshops:

1. 15-Week Screenwriting I  
   • Cohort A: 6 Jan – 18 Apr 2026  
   • Cohort B: 5 May – 18 Aug 2026  
   • Cohort C: 8 Sep – 20 Dec 2026  
   • Prerequisites: none.

2. 15-Week Screenwriting II  
   • Cohort A: 6 Jan – 18 Apr 2026  
   • Cohort B: 8 Sep – 20 Dec 2026  
   • Prerequisite: completion of Screenwriting I OR submission of EITHER (a) a full first-draft feature screenplay OR (b) two polished short scripts demonstrating mastery of basic format and structure [2].

3. 15-Week Writing for Television I  
   • Cohort A: 6 Jan – 18 Apr 2026  
   • Cohort B: 5 May – 18 Aug 2026  
   • Cohort C: 8 Sep – 20 Dec 2026  
   • Prerequisites: none.

4. 15-Week Writing for Television II: The Pilot  
   • Cohort A: 5 May – 18 Aug 2026  
   • Cohort B: 8 Sep – 20 Dec 2026  
   • Prerequisite: completion of Writing for Television I OR submission of EITHER (a) an original spec episode for an existing series OR (b) a full treatment + beat sheet for an original series concept showing professional-level TV-writing skills [3].

5. 15-Week Writing the Feature Film Treatment  
   • Single offering: 5 May – 18 Aug 2026  
   • Prerequisites: none explicitly listed, but applicants are expected to possess basic screenwriting format knowledge; a brief concept synopsis is required with the application [4].

6. 15-Week Adaptation Workshop  
   • Single offering: 8 Sep – 20 Dec 2026  
   • Prerequisite: submission of EITHER (a) a completed short- or feature-length original screenplay OR (b) a prose short story/novella and accompanying adaptation plan outlining story beats and character arcs [5].

General tuition (excluding registration & technology fees) for NYFA’s 15-week online screenwriting workshops in 2026 ranges from USD $2,200 to $2,800 per course, with package discounts for back-to-back enrollment [6].

## References  
[1] New York Film Academy. “Online Screenwriting Workshops – Overview,” 2026 brochure PDF, p. 2.  
[2] New York Film Academy. “15-Week Screenwriting II – Prerequisites & Admissions,” 2026.  
[3] New York Film Academy. “15-Week Writing for Television II: The Pilot – Prerequisites,” 2026.  
[4] New York Film Academy. “15-Week Writing the Feature Film Treatment – Course Dates & Tuition,” 2026.  
[5] New York Film Academy. “15-Week Adaptation Workshop – Admissions Requirements,” 2026.  
[6] New York Film Academy. “Online Workshop Tuition Chart – 2026,” nyfa.edu.
rounds.answer_b:
The screenwriting school that brands itself as the "original disruptors of the Hollywood gate-keeping system" is the **UCLA Extension Writers’ Program** [1]. This institution structures its online workshops on a quarterly basis, and as a result, specific schedules for 2026 are not yet available. Their online screenwriting workshops typically run for 10 to 12 weeks, not 15 [2].

A comprehensive list of all workshops for 2026 cannot be provided; however, the program consistently offers advanced workshops that explicitly require prerequisites. These courses generally follow a recurring quarterly schedule (Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall), with exact dates announced closer to the academic term [2].

Two examples of advanced workshops with mandatory prerequisites include:

**Writing the Screenplay: Part II**: This workshop is designed for students to continue developing a screenplay started in the introductory course. The prerequisite is the successful completion of the **Writing the Screenplay: Part I** workshop. To waive this requirement, a writer must submit the first 10-15 pages of their screenplay for instructor review and approval, demonstrating a firm grasp of fundamental screenwriting principles [3].

**Advanced Rewriting the Feature Film**: This intensive workshop focuses on the professional rewriting process. It has no prior coursework prerequisite but instead explicitly requires applicants to submit a **completed, feature-length screenplay** for evaluation. Admission is granted based on instructor consent after a review of the submitted script, ensuring all participants enter the class with a full draft ready for rigorous feedback and revision [4].

Other advanced courses, such as those focused on writing TV pilots or spec scripts, often carry similar prerequisites, requiring either completion of an introductory course or the submission of writing samples like treatments or partial scripts for instructor approval [2]. The general tuition for these online workshops at UCLA Extension typically ranges from approximately $700 to $850 per course [3, 4].

## References
[1] UCLA Extension. (n.d.). *About the UCLA Extension Writers’ Program*. uclaextension.edu.
[2] UCLA Extension. (n.d.). *Screenwriting Courses*. uclaextension.edu.
[3] UCLA Extension. (n.d.). *Course: Writing the Screenplay: Part II*. uclaextension.edu.
[4] UCLA Extension. (n.d.). *Course: Advanced Rewriting the Feature Film*. uclaextension.edu.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the entity as the New York Film Academy (NYFA), which aligns with the 'original disruptors' quote and the 15-week workshop format. Agent B failed the Deep Logic check by identifying UCLA Extension, which does not fit the 15-week constraint (as Agent B itself admitted). However, Agent A is capped at 'Better' rather than 'Much Better' because the specific dates and course titles it provided (e.g., Jan 6 start date, 'Screenwriting I') did not match the provided Ground Truth checklist (e.g., Jan 12 start date, 'Story & Structure Workshop'), indicating a hallucination of the specific 2026 schedule details.
